description_long: This object consists of a tightly packed rectangular array of identical clear plastic water bottles, each with a blue-tinted cap. The bottles are arranged upright in rows and columns, forming a block-like structure. Each bottle is cylindrical with a slightly tapered neck and a visible screw cap, and the transparent plastic reveals the clear liquid inside. The caps are uniformly colored, providing a visual contrast to the otherwise colorless bottles. The bottles appear to be standard single-serve size, commonly found in multipacks for retail or event use. The overall appearance is clean, modern, and utilitarian, suitable for urban distribution or storage.
description: A rectangular multipack of clear plastic water bottles with blue caps, arranged in neat rows. The bottles are transparent and filled with water, forming a compact, block-like bundle.
description_view_0: Front view showing the long side of the multipack, with all bottle caps aligned in a row.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the short edge of the pack, highlighting the depth and the alignment of bottle necks.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing the opposite long side of the multipack.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the short edge and the uniformity of the bottle arrangement.
attribute_cap_type: screw cap; short, cylindrical, blue, ribbed for grip
attribute_transparency: fully transparent bottle body with slight blue tint; no patterns or gradients
attribute_bottle_neck_design: short, slightly tapered neck with external threads for screw cap; ribbed texture near the top
